demographics.living_situation: lives at home with both parents

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: His nose will not stop pouring blood — he's soaked through three thick towels, and now he says the room is spinning. (parent calling on behalf of her 7-year-old son)
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The nosebleed has been going for about 45 minutes and pinching hasn't helped at all; he has a genetic condition and a bleeding problem, and he's gone pale and dizzy.

symptom_narrative.onset: about 45 minutes ago, came on suddenly with no obvious injury
symptom_narrative.character: heavy, continuous bleeding pouring from the right nostril, dark red
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst nosebleed he's ever had — it has soaked through three thick towels
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: right nostril; some blood is also trickling down the back of his throat
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since it started, not letting up
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing — we pinched the bridge of his nose and it didn't slow it down
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it just keeps flowing on its own
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: over the last 15 minutes he has gone pale, become a bit floppy and sleepy, and says he feels dizzy and that the room spins when he sits up
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: he has had nosebleeds before because of his condition, but never one this heavy or this long
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: he is lying flat now because he gets dizzy when he sits up; too unwell to move around

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: Noonan syndrome (a genetic condition he was born with)
past_medical_history.how_managed: followed by paediatric and specialist teams

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a bleeding problem — the doctors told us he is missing a clotting factor (Factor XI)
past_medical_history.how_managed: known bleeding tendency, monitored by a blood specialist

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a heart valve problem (narrowing of the valve to the lungs) with a murmur
he is small for his age and has some learning difficulties
past_medical_history.how_managed: supported at school and by his paediatrician

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: a nose gel / medicine for bleeds (tranexamic acid) that we were given before

family_history_as_reported.narrative: his genetic condition runs in families; we were told bleeding can be part of it

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: 7-year-old at home with both parents; parents present and made the call

paediatric_context.developmental_concerns: known short stature and mild learning difficulties associated with his genetic condition

objective_data_offered.value: child looks very pale, is lying flat, and has bright/dark red blood actively running from the right nostril onto a towel
he has soaked through three thick towels and there is a big pool of blood on the floor
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: carer estimate of volume, not measured

objective_data_offered.value: he has gone floppy and sleepy over the last 15 minutes and says he feels dizzy and the room spins when he sits up
some blood is trickling down the back of his throat and he keeps swallowing it
